articles.title: 'Controversial' North Korean invasion setting for next Call of Duty game
articles.summary: Developer Infinity Ward said the game will be "grounded in the military authenticity" the series is known for.
articles.titleZh: 下一款《使命召唤》游戏的朝鲜入侵设定“有争议”
articles.summaryZh: 开发商 Infinity Ward 表示，这款游戏将“植根于该系列闻名的军事真实性”。

contentParagraphs.en: 'Controversial' North Korean invasion setting for next Call of Duty game20 hours agoLaura CressTechnology reporterInfinity Ward / ActivisionThe next Call of Duty game has been revealed, with much of the reaction focused on its campaign set around a fictional renewed conflict on the Korean Peninsula.Modern Warfare 4, due out 23 October, partly follows South Korean soldiers battling a full-scale North Korean invasion.Dr Sarah Son, Senior Lecturer in Korean Studies at the University of Sheffield, said the move "could be controversial" as it "turns still-unresolved war into entertainment". Some Koreans reacted more positively, with one calling Korea's inclusion in one of gaming's biggest franchises a "symbolic moment".Developer Infinity Ward said the game will be "grounded in the military authenticity Modern Warfare is known for".The game will launch on current-generation consoles, PC and Nintendo Switch 2, marking the first mainline Call of Duty to skip PlayStation 4 and Xbox One.The game's trailer, which has already been viewed almost 22 million times within a day of release, focuses on a group of young South Korean conscripts on what appears to be a routine patrol, before a missile attack from North Korea throws them into full-scale war.Players will also get to play once again as fan-favourite Captain Price, who will appear in different missions in several cities alongside the Korean campaign.Infinity Ward/ ActivisionThe release of any Call of Duty game is a global cultural event and posts about the latest version have amassed more than three million interactions within 24 hours of the announcement across Instagram, TikTok, X and Facebook.Among them, some Koreans reacting to the setting have embraced Infinity Ward's decision to tell the story from the perspective of ordinary South Korean soldiers caught up in the conflict."The soldiers' faces and the atmosphere of the locations all have that familiar Korean feel, so I'm genuinely excited," said one."When I heard the rumour that the ROK Army would be in it, my immediate reaction was 'obviously just an extra...'," posted another. "Then I heard they're not just present but one of the playable protagonists? And not even special forces, handled from the perspective of an ordinary conscripted soldier, that's what gets me."Beyond the setting, Infinity Ward announced significant changes to gameplay, including revamped movement mechanics and more interactive environments.The studio is also overhauling DMZ, its extraction-style multiplayer mode, and introducing a new 'Frontlines' system designed to make battles feel more dynamic and reactive.Previous controversiesModern Warfare has previously courted controversy through storylines inspired by real-world events and conflicts.Missions such as "No Russian", where players had the option to shoot civilians in an airport in Moscow, and later depictions of war crimes and terrorism have prompted debate about how far games should go in portraying realistic warfare.Dr Son said while the idea of a renewed inter-Korean conflict is "not unheard of" in Korean popular culture, these stories were often told "from a South Korean perspective"."A global gaming franchise might be judged differently," she said.The Korean War ended in an armistice rather than a peace treaty in 1953, meaning North and South Korea remain technically at war.George Osborn, author of "Power Play: Video Games, Politics and the Battle for Global Influence" told the BBC the setting was "likely to attract scrutiny" in the territory and pointed to previous games, such as Homefront, which depicted a unified Korea under northern control and which had received bans in South Korea."The studio will have to show that it has handled possible conflict in the country with great care, or face significant backlash – and possible challenges selling the game – in South Korea specifically," he added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 下一款《使命召唤》游戏的“有争议的”朝鲜入侵设定 20 小时前，劳拉·克雷斯 (Laura Cress) 科技记者 Infinity Ward / Activision 下一款《使命召唤》游戏已经揭晓，大部分反应都集中在围绕朝鲜半岛虚构的新冲突展开的战役上。《现代战争 4》将于 10 月 23 日推出，部分讲述了韩国士兵与朝鲜全面入侵作战的故事。谢菲尔德表示，此举“可能会引起争议”，因为它“将尚未解决的战争变成了娱乐”。一些韩国人的反应更为积极，有人称韩国加入游戏最大的特许经营权之一是一个“象征性时刻”。开发商 Infinity Ward 表示，该游戏将“以《现代战争》闻名的军事真实性为基础”。该游戏将在当前一代游戏机、PC 和 Nintendo Switch 2 上发布，标志着第一款跳过 PlayStation 4 和 Xbox One 的主线《使命召唤》。该游戏的预告片一天内浏览量已近 2200 万次游戏的重点是一群年轻的韩国应征入伍者，在朝鲜的导弹袭击将他们卷入全面战争之前，他们似乎正在进行例行巡逻。玩家还将再次扮演粉丝最喜欢的普莱斯上尉，他将与朝鲜战役一起出现在多个城市的不同任务中。Infinity Ward/动视任何一款《使命召唤》游戏的发布都是一项全球文化活动，有关最新版本的帖子在发布后 24 小时内就积累了超过 300 万次互动。 Instagram、TikTok、X 和 Facebook。其中，一些对场景做出反应的韩国人对 Infinity Ward 决定从陷入冲突的普通韩国士兵的角度来讲述这个故事表示欢迎。“士兵的脸和场景的气氛都有那种熟悉的韩国感觉，所以我真的很兴奋，”一位网友说道。“当我听到韩国军队将出现在其中的传言时，我的第一反应是‘显然只是一个额外的……’”，另一位网友写道。 “然后我听说他们不仅在场，而且是可玩的主角之一？甚至连特种部队都没有，从普通应征士兵的角度来处理，这就是让我着迷的地方。”除了背景之外，Infinity Ward 还宣布了游戏玩法的重大变化，包括改进的运动机制和更多的互动环境。该工作室还在彻底修改 DMZ，其提取式多人游戏模式，并引入了新的“前线”系统，旨在让战斗感觉更加动态和先前的争议《现代战争》此前曾因受现实世界事件和冲突启发的故事情节而引起争议。诸如“禁止俄罗斯人”之类的任务，玩家可以选择在莫斯科机场射击平民，后来对战争罪行和恐怖主义的描述引发了关于游戏应在多大程度上描绘现实战争的争论。孙博士表示，虽然重新爆发韩朝冲突的想法在韩国流行文化中“并非闻所未闻”，但这些故事通常是“从南方讲述的”她说：“对全球游戏系列的评价可能会有所不同。”1953 年，朝鲜战争以停战而非和平条约结束，这意味着朝鲜和韩国在技术上仍处于战争状态。他补充道：“工作室必须证明，它已经非常谨慎地处理了该国可能发生的冲突，否则将面临严重的抵制，以及在韩国销售游戏可能面临的挑战。”

articles.title: Bean plants call for aerial reinforcements when caterpillars attack
articles.summary: NPR's Short Wave talks about a weakness in a well-known insect repellant, how plants call wasps to their defense and how bigger rewards speed up learning, in mice.
articles.titleZh: 当毛毛虫袭击时，豆类植物需要空中增援
articles.summaryZh: NPR 的《短波》讨论了一种著名的驱虫剂的弱点、植物如何召唤黄蜂进行防御，以及更大的奖励如何加速老鼠的学习速度。

contentParagraphs.en: NPR's Short Wave talks about a weakness in a well-known insect repellant, how plants call wasps to their defense and how bigger rewards speed up learning, in mice.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的《短波》讨论了一种著名的驱虫剂的弱点、植物如何召唤黄蜂进行防御，以及更大的奖励如何加速老鼠的学习速度。

contentParagraphs.en: SACHA PFEIFFER, HOST: It's time now for our science news roundup from Short Wave, NPR's science podcast. And I'm joined by Regina Barber and Hannah Chinn. Hi to both of you.REGINA BARBER, BYLINE: Hey.HANNAH CHINN, BYLINE: Hey.PFEIFFER: You have brought us three science stories that caught your attention this week. What are the three?BARBER: So bean plants' surprising chemical counterattack against pests.CHINN: We've got new ways to speed up learning in mice.BARBER: And how our strongest mosquito repellent may have a weakness we didn't know about before.PFEIFFER: Ah. So since fiber is such a wellness trend right now...BARBER: Yes.PFEIFFER: I keep hearing about fibermaxxing.BARBER: Yes.PFEIFFER: Why don't we start with the bean? Tell us about that one.BARBER: Yeah. So this story is an amazing example of plant versus animal warfare, and it might make you think a little differently about how crafty plants can be in defending themselves. And it all centers around the common bean plant. So this is the one I feel like a lot of grade schoolers grow in classrooms. It's a garden staple, the bush bean or pole bean. And it turns out it has an elaborate chemical defense mechanism.PFEIFFER: Ah, and what's it defending itself against? I assume some pest or some blight or something?CHINN: You're right. In this case, it's caterpillars. So, Sacha, in a study out this week in the journal Science Advances, researchers described how when caterpillars munch on bean plants, a compound in the caterpillar spit causes the bean plants to release a chemical signal into the air.PFEIFFER: (Laughter) OK.CHINN: Think about it as a kind of distress flare that calls in a fleet of wasp bodyguards.ADAM STEINBRENNER: The types of chemicals we were able to measure were known to be attractants to predatory wasps, parasitoid wasps that lay eggs in caterpillars.CHINN: So this is Adam Steinbrenner. He's a plant biologist at the University of Washington, and he's one of the authors on the study. He says some of the wasps eat the caterpillars. Others lay eggs inside the caterpillars' bodies so their babies can eat them, really effectively removing the threat to the bean plants.BARBER: And, Sacha, this kind of chemical signal isn't rare. Plants release them all the time. Like, cut grass smells a specific way. That's the plant's chemical response to being wounded.PFEIFFER: Interesting. The cut grass smelling a specific way reminds me of flowers smelling sweet because they're trying to attract bees.BARBER: Yeah.CHINN: Yeah. And these are both chemical responses, right? But the specific compound that attracts wasps is really only triggered via caterpillar spit. So the bean plant doesn't call wasps in if, say, you cut a leaf or if you damage the plant in a different way. And, Sacha, this is just one of multiple ways that plants have really evolved to defend themselves, if you think about the thorns of a rose or the spines of a cactus or bitter, poisonous leaves. Or I guess, in this case, this strategy - right? - calling in some aerial reinforcements from the insect world.PFEIFFER: It's like calling in the drones.CHINN: Yes. Yes.BARBER: (Laughter) Exactly.PFEIFFER: All right. You also mentioned, for your second topic, new ways to speed up learning in mice. Tell us about that one.BARBER: Yeah, so I brought you a study just published in the journal Science. In lab experiments, researchers typically give little rewards to help them learn tasks. But in this study, they found that giving them bigger rewards less frequently actually helped the mice learn a lot faster. Here's Josh Dudman, one of the authors of the study.JOSH DUDMAN: We discovered that we had been substantially underestimating how efficiently animals can learn. We could essentially take some of the slowest learners and move them all the way up to the fastest learners.PFEIFFER: I really want to know what the rewards were. I'm thinking about cheese or peanut butter we put in a mouse trap. What did they use as rewards for these mice?CHINN: So in this case, the reward was artificially sweetened water, which, for what it's worth, is also something that humans appreciate.PFEIFFER: And what sort of things were the mice trying to learn?CHINN: Well, one was the classic Pavlovian cue. They were learning that certain sounds meant a reward was coming. And they also had to learn harder things, like pulling a joystick or turning a tiny steering wheel right or left to get a reward.PFEIFFER: So I'm interested in this idea that a bigger amount of reward given less frequently resulted in faster learning rather than smaller, more frequent rewards. Tell us more about why that worked better.CHINN: Yeah. Well, we don't know the whole picture, but Josh and his colleagues think it has something to do with dopamine and engagement, right? Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good, and then your brain is like, oh, I want to do that again. And when the mice got these big rewards, they got a bigger burst of dopamine. And the scientists say that lasted longer and kept them more engaged than these little rewards. They also pointed out there might be other factors at play, not just dopamine.PFEIFFER: Since this is just a mouse study, what does it tell us potentially about human learning?BARBER: Yeah, so these experiments give insight into what keeps the brain engaged, which might be helpful for future animal experiments. We aren't quite at the point of comparing these experiments to human learning, but it's a step in the right direction.PFEIFFER: All right, so topic three, we move on to another type of pest in some people's eyes, and that is how our strongest mosquito repellent may have a weakness we didn't know about before. What repellent, and what weakness?BARBER: Yeah, so many people know DEET is an important chemical in many mosquito r
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人萨查·普法伊弗 (SACHA PFEIFFER)：现在是 NPR 科学播客短波的科学新闻综述时间。雷吉娜·巴伯 (Regina Barber) 和汉娜·钦 (Hannah Chinn) 也加入了我的行列。大家好。雷吉娜·巴伯连线：嘿。汉娜·钦连线：嘿。普法伊弗：本周你给我们带来了三个引起你注意的科学故事。三者分别是哪三个？ 理发师：所以豆类植物对害虫具有惊人的化学反击能力。钦恩：我们已经找到了加快老鼠学习速度的新方法。理发师：我们最强的驱蚊剂可能有一个我们以前不知道的弱点。普法伊弗：啊。因此，既然纤维现在是一种健康趋势...理发师：是的。普法伊弗：我一直听说纤维最大。理发师：是的。普法伊弗：我们为什么不从豆子开始呢？给我们讲一下那个吧。 理发师：是的。所以这个故事是植物与动物战争的一个惊人的例子，它可能会让你对狡猾的植物如何保护自己有不同的看法。这一切都以普通豆类植物为中心。所以我觉得这是很多小学生在教室里成长的。它是花园里的主食，灌木豆或扁豆。事实证明它有一个复杂的化学防御机制。 PFEIFFER：啊，它在防御什么？我认为是某种害虫或枯萎病之类的？CHINN：你是对的。在这种情况下，它是毛毛虫。因此，Sacha 在本周发表在《科学进展》杂志上的一项研究中，研究人员描述了当毛毛虫咀嚼豆类植物时，毛毛虫吐出的一种化合物会导致豆类植物向空气中释放化学信号。 PFEIFFER：（笑声）好的。CHINN：将其视为一种召唤黄蜂保镖的求救信号弹。ADAM STEINBRENNER：我们能够测量的化学物质类型是捕食性黄蜂、在毛毛虫体内产卵的寄生黄蜂的引诱剂。CHINN：这是亚当·斯坦布伦纳（Adam Steinbrenner）。他是华盛顿大学的植物生物学家，也是该研究的作者之一。他说有些黄蜂会吃毛毛虫。其他的则在毛毛虫体内产卵，这样它们的幼虫就可以吃掉它们，这确实有效地消除了对豆类植物的威胁。 理发师：而且，萨莎，这种化学信号并不罕见。植物一直在释放它们。就像，割下的草有一种特殊的气味。这就是植物对受伤时的化学反应。 PFEIFFER：有趣。割下来的草闻起来有一种特殊的味道，让我想起闻起来很甜的花，因为它们试图吸引蜜蜂。理发师：是的。钦恩：是的。这些都是化学反应，对吗？但吸引黄蜂的特定化合物实际上只能通过毛毛虫的唾液触发。因此，如果你割下一片叶子或者以不同的方式损坏植物，豆类植物不会召唤黄蜂。而且，萨沙，这只是植物真正进化来保护自己的多种方式之一，如果你想想玫瑰的刺、仙人掌的刺或苦涩有毒的叶子。或者我猜，在这种情况下，这个策略 - 对吧？ - 从昆虫世界呼叫一些空中增援。 PFEIFFER：这就像呼叫无人机。CHINN：是的。是的。 理发师：（笑声）完全正确。 普法伊弗：好的。对于第二个主题，您还提到了加速小鼠学习的新方法。给我们讲一下这个吧。 理发师：是的，我给你带来了一项刚刚发表在《科学》杂志上的研究。在实验室实验中，研究人员通常给予很少的奖励来帮助他们学习任务。但在这项研究中，他们发现，较少给予小鼠更大的奖励实际上可以帮助小鼠学得更快。请听该研究的作者之一乔什·杜德曼 (Josh Dudman) 的报道。 乔什·杜德曼 (Josh Dudman)：我们发现我们一直低估了动物的学习效率。我们基本上可以将一些最慢的学习者提升到最快的学习者。 PFEIFFER：我真的很想知道奖励是什么。我正在考虑我们放入捕鼠器中的奶酪或花生酱。他们用什么作为这些老鼠的奖励？CHINN：所以在这种情况下，奖励是人工加糖的水，就其价值而言，这也是人类欣赏的东西。 PFEIFFER：老鼠试图学习什么样的东西？CHINN：嗯，其中一个是经典的巴甫洛夫暗示。他们了解到某些声音意味着奖励即将到来。他们还必须学习更难的事情，比如拉动操纵杆或向右或向左转动小方向盘来获得奖励。 PFEIFFER：所以我对这个想法很感兴趣，即大量、不频繁的奖励会导致更快的学习，而不是更小、更频繁的奖励。告诉我们更多关于为什么效果更好的信息。CHINN：是的。好吧，我们不知道整个情况，但乔什和他的同事认为这与多巴胺和参与度有关，对吗？多巴胺是一种让你感觉良好的化学物质，然后你的大脑就会说，哦，我想再这样做一次。当小鼠获得这些巨大的奖励时，它们会产生更大量的多巴胺。科学家们表示，与这些小奖励相比，这种奖励持续时间更长，也让他们更加投入。他们还指出，可能还有其他因素在起作用，而不仅仅是多巴胺。 PFEIFFER：既然这只是一项小鼠研究，它能告诉我们关于人类学习的什么潜在信息？ BARBER：是的，所以这些实验深入了解了是什么让大脑保持活跃，这可能对未来的动物实验有帮助。我们还没有完全达到将这些实验与人类学习进行比较的阶段，但这是朝着正确方向迈出的一步。 PFEIFFER：好的，所以主题三，我们继续讨论一些人眼中的另一种害虫，这就是我们最强的驱蚊剂可能有一个我们以前不知道的弱点。什么驱虫剂，什么弱点？理发师：是的，很多人都知道避蚊胺是许多蚊子体内的重要化学物质。

articles.summaryZh:
《侠盗一号》电影制片人加雷斯·爱德华兹 (Gareth Edwards) 在亚马逊的 AI on the Lot 活动上发表讲话时表示，“它会做你要求的任何事情”并且“它会比 CGI 更好”
《侏罗纪世界重生》和《侠盗一号》导演加雷斯·爱德华兹热情支持在电影中使用生成式人工智能

contentParagraphs.en: Jurassic World Rebirth and Rogue One director Gareth Edwards has enthusiastically endorsed the use of generative AI in film-making, saying “it is a fucking genius at helping you” and “it’s going to be better than CGI”.Edwards was speaking at AI on the Lot, an event in Culver City, California, organised by Amazon, and in remarks reported by the Hollywood Reporter said: “I can’t see a reason why you wouldn’t become interested in this stuff as a film-maker. It’s so clearly a tool that might be up there with the camera. It’s going to be better than CGI.”Edwards said that AI is most useful in the preparatory stages of film-making, saying: “It’s only good for iteration and discovering what the movie should be, and then once you know what it is, go in and start making it your movie.”He added: “It has no taste whatsoever. It is a fucking genius at helping you. I view it like having a second-unit director who is a billionaire on acid. Like, it’ll do anything you ask, not a problem. Sometimes, it’ll [go] batshit crazy. And you’ll give it notes, and it’ll be like, ‘I don’t do notes. I’ll just do something totally different.’ But it’s worth it.”Edwards’ positive view of AI was echoed by veteran writer and director Paul Schrader, who was also speaking at the event. In remarks reported by Deadline, Schrader said: “I don’t think the real future of AI commercially is in all this flash, all these monsters – that’s just jacked-up special effects on steroids,” he said. “The real tip of the spear is when we can create an AI protagonist, not a hybrid, and that movie makes money. When you do the new Clint Eastwood, but you don’t say the words ‘Clint Eastwood’ to AI, you just describe him. And he comes up as Clint Eastwood.”Schrader added: “And the movie comes out, and us carbon-based fools spend our money empathising and caring about silicon-based creations.”Schrader, director of American Gigolo and First Reformed as well as the writer of the Martin Scorsese-directed Taxi Driver, said that extras were also no longer needed. “Why are we paying extras $180 a day when they look so plastic? We not only pay them, we have to clothe them and feed them. Why don’t we just make them? We can and we will.”So far, films using gen AI images have not gone down well with critics, including Steven Soderbergh’s documentary John Lennon: The Last Interview, whose AI inserts the Guardian’s Peter Bradshaw described as “blandly generic and very mediocre”.Edwards, however, said that the pace of change in AI technology meant that it was impossible to predict its future. “We don’t know where it’s going to go. I think anybody saying they know exactly what’s going to happen over the next five years is just a liar.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 《侏罗纪世界重生》和《侠盗一号》的导演加雷斯·爱德华兹 (Gareth Edwards) 热情支持在电影制作中使用生成式人工智能，他说“这真是一个能帮助你的天才”，“它会比 CGI 更好”。 爱德华兹在亚马逊在加利福尼亚州卡尔弗城举办的 AI on the Lot 活动上发表讲话，并在《好莱坞报道》报道的评论中说道：“我看不出你有什么理由不对这个东西感兴趣，因为很明显，它是一种比 CGI 更好的工具。”爱德华兹说，人工智能在电影制作的准备阶段最有用，他说：“它只适合迭代和发现电影应该是什么，然后一旦你知道它是什么，就开始制作它。”他补充道：“它毫无品味，就像在帮助你一样。就像，它会做你要求的任何事情，这不是问题。有时，你会给它做笔记，而你会说，‘我不做笔记，我只会做一些完全不同的事情。’但这是值得的。”爱德华兹对人工智能的积极看法得到了也在活动上发言的资深作家兼导演保罗·施拉德的赞同。在 Deadline 报道的评论中，施拉德表示：“我认为商业人工智能的真正未来并不在于所有这些闪光、所有这些怪物——那只是类固醇上的特效，”他说。 “真正的矛尖是当我们能够创造一个人工智能主角，而不是混合体，那部电影就能赚钱。当你制作新的克林特·伊斯特伍德时，但你不会对人工智能说‘克林特·伊斯特伍德’这个词，你只是描述他。他就会以克林特·伊斯特伍德的身份出现。”施拉德补充道：“电影出来了，我们碳基傻瓜花我们的钱来同情和关心硅基创作。”施拉德，美国导演舞男和第一归正会以及马丁·斯科塞斯执导的《出租车司机》的编剧表示，也不再需要额外的演员了。 “为什么我们要每天额外支付 180 美元，它们看起来那么塑料？我们不仅付钱给它们，我们还得给它们穿衣服、喂它们。为什么我们不直接制作它们呢？我们可以而且我们会的。”到目前为止，使用新一代人工智能图像的电影并没有受到评论家的好评，包括史蒂文·索德伯格的纪录片《约翰·列侬：最后的采访》，其人工智能插入了《卫报》的彼得·布拉德肖（Peter Bradshaw）描述为“平淡通用且非常然而，爱德华兹表示，人工智能技术的变化速度意味着无法预测其未来。 “我们不知道它会走向何方。我认为任何说他们确切知道未来五年会发生什么的人都是骗子。”

articles.title: Here's how we're coping with high gas prices, according to Costco and Walmart
articles.summary: Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.
articles.titleZh: Costco 和 Walmart 表示，以下是我们应对高油价的方法
articles.summaryZh: 天价的汽油价格让司机们不遗余力地在加油站寻求折扣。石油高管警告称，油价可能会更高。

contentParagraphs.en: Drivers fuel up at a Costco gas station in Roseville, Mich.
contentParagraphs.zh: 司机们在密歇根州罗斯维尔的 Costco 加油站加油。

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter sent every weekday morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 请随时关注我们每个工作日早上发送的 Up First 时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: In the hunt for cheaper gas, people are going out of their way for discounted fuel at Costco and Walmart — many of them visiting for the first time. Drivers also are now filling up more often, but topping up with fewer gallons at a time.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了寻找更便宜的汽油，人们不遗余力地在好市多和沃尔玛购买打折燃油——其中许多人是第一次来。驾驶员现在也更频繁地加油，但一次加油的加仑数减少了。

contentParagraphs.en: As the U.S. war in Iran continues to drive up gas prices, Costco's gas stations have been selling record amounts of fuel, executives told investors on a call Thursday. In fact, Costco had never sold as much gas as it did between April and mid-May, CEO Ron Vachris said, with stations having to get multiple daily gas deliveries to keep up. People have been willing to drive further and wait in line longer to buy cheaper gas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 高管们在周四的电话会议上告诉投资者，随着美国在伊朗的战争继续推高汽油价格，好市多的加油站销售的燃料数量创下了纪录。首席执行官罗恩·瓦克里斯 (Ron Vachris) 表示，事实上，Costco 在 4 月至 5 月中旬期间售出的天然气量从未如此之多，加油站必须每天进行多次天然气输送才能跟上。人们愿意开得更远，排队更长的时间来购买更便宜的汽油。

contentParagraphs.en: "A lot of members are increasing their frequency of visiting the gas station to top up in between what would have normally been a gap between getting the tank to empty because of the concern about what might the gas price be tomorrow," said Gary Millerchip, Costco's finance chief.
contentParagraphs.zh: Costco 财务主管加里·米勒奇普 (Gary Millerchip) 表示：“由于担心明天的汽油价格，许多会员正在增加前往加油站加油的频率，因为担心明天的汽油价格可能会出现变化。”

contentParagraphs.en: At Walmart's gas stations, in recent weeks, people have begun to fill up with fewer than ten gallons at a time, for the first time since 2022.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在沃尔玛的加油站，最近几周，人们开始一次加油不足10加仑，这是自2022年以来的首次。

contentParagraphs.en: "That's an indication of stress," Chief Financial Officer John David Rainey told investors last week.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这是压力的迹象，”首席财务官约翰·大卫·雷尼上周告诉投资者。

contentParagraphs.en: "We see with our customers that the high-income customer is spending with confidence," Rainey added later, "while the lower-income consumer is more budget-conscious and perhaps navigating financial distress."
contentParagraphs.zh: 雷尼后来补充道：“我们从客户身上看到，高收入客户消费信心十足，而低收入消费者则更加注重预算，或许还面临着财务困境。”

contentParagraphs.en: Similarly, the discount gas-station chain Murphy USA also told investors in late April that it had started to draw more first-time shoppers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 同样，折扣加油站连锁店Murphy USA也在4月底告诉投资者，它已经开始吸引更多的首次购物者。

contentParagraphs.en: "We're also seeing lapsed customers returning to our stores," said CEO Mindy West. "They're changing their behavior and becoming more value-seeking shoppers."
contentParagraphs.zh: 首席执行官明迪·韦斯特 (Mindy West) 表示：“我们还看到流失的顾客返回我们的商店。” “他们正在改变自己的行为，成为更加追求价值的购物者。”

contentParagraphs.en: The average U.S. price of regular gas on Thursday was $4.39 per gallon, according to AAA. That's up $1.22 from a year ago. This has Americans devoting a greater share of their paycheck to gas. The latest federal data shows that spending at gas stations in April surged a whopping 21% compared to last year.
contentParagraphs.zh: The average U.S. price of regular gas on Thursday was $4.39 per gallon, according to AAA.比一年前上涨了 1.22 美元。这使得美国人将更大比例的薪水用于天然气。最新的联邦数据显示，4 月份加油站的支出比去年猛增 21%。

contentParagraphs.en: A growing chorus is warning of even higher prices to come — potentially within weeks — as global oil supplies are depleting. Oil tankers have been blocked from passing through the Strait of Hormuz near Iran, a vital corridor for global fuel shipments.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着全球石油供应不断枯竭，越来越多的人警告油价可能会在几周内上涨。油轮无法通过伊朗附近的霍尔木兹海峡，该海峡是全球燃料运输的重要走廊。

contentParagraphs.en: At an investment conference on Thursday, a top ExxonMobil executive said that prices could jump by an "order of magnitude" in a matter of weeks, as oil reserves reach "unheard-of inventory levels... really, really low levels," according to Oil & Gas Journal.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据《石油与天然气杂志》报道，在周四的投资会议上，埃克森美孚的一位高管表示，随着石油储备达到“闻所未闻的库存水平……非常非常低的水平”，价格可能在几周内上涨“一个数量级”。

contentParagraphs.en: Over time, the higher gas prices will also lead to higher prices on store shelves, according to executives at both Walmart and Costco. Rising fuel costs are adding up for companies that transport a lot, including manufacturers and retailers. Plus, the Strait of Hormuz is also a critical route for shipments of fertilizer needed to grow food. And Costco's Millerchip said the cost of petroleum has driven up the cost of resin, which is likely to increase the price of items using plastic or polyester.
contentParagraphs.zh: 沃尔玛和好市多的高管表示，随着时间的推移，汽油价格上涨也将导致商店货架上的价格上涨。对于包括制造商和零售商在内的运输量大的公司来说，不断上涨的燃料成本正在增加。此外，霍尔木兹海峡也是运输粮食种植所需化肥的重要路线。 Costco的Millerchip表示，石油成本推高了树脂成本，这可能会提高使用塑料或聚酯的物品的价格。

articles.title: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
articles.summary: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.
articles.titleZh: 火箭爆炸让人们对美国宇航局登月计划产生怀疑
articles.summaryZh: 蓝色起源火箭的爆炸对该公司和美国宇航局的月球计划来说都是一次挫折。

contentParagraphs.en: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans23 hours agoPallab GhoshScience correspondentThe fireball that lit up the sky over Florida's Kennedy Space Centre last night has put a big question mark over whether Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin can deliver on a string of commitments to Nasa in its efforts to send astronauts to the lunar surface and build a Moon base.Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded at about 21:00 local time during a routine test of its engines.The 98m (322ft) rocket had been due to launch 48 satellites for Amazon's Leo broadband network, as early as 4 June.The explosion is obviously a big setback for the Leo network, which is struggling to be the main competitor for Elon Musk's SpaceX and its Starlink service. But the ramifications will go much further.The good news was that no-one was injured, despite the spectacular explosion."All personnel are accounted for and safe," Bezos wrote on X. "Very rough day, but we'll rebuild whatever needs rebuilding and get back to flying. It's worth it."But the blast which tore through Space Launch Complex 36 (LC-36) has caused extensive damage. Footage shows one of the pad's lightning protection towers toppling in the aftermath.LC-36 is the only facility in the world built to launch the New Glenn rocket. That means that until the launch pad is rebuilt and re-certified, Blue Origin has no way to fly its largest rocket - and analysts expect that to take months, not weeks.The setback comes just days after Nasa's administrator, Jared Isaacman, announced the first three missions of the agency's plans to build a lunar base - a project he billed as the start of a "permanent presence" at the Moon's south pole.The first, Moon Base 1, is due to be flown on Blue Origin's robotic Blue Moon Mark 1 "Endurance" lander, and is targeted for launch no earlier than autumn 2026.It is intended to carry two Nasa science payloads to the Shackleton Connecting Ridge and demonstrate the precision-landing techniques needed to keep future crewed landings safe. But the lander was to ride to the Moon on top of a New Glenn - the same type of rocket that is now scattered across LC-36 - raising immediate doubts as to whether that timetable is now possible.Earlier this week, Nasa also handed Blue Origin a contract worth up to $468m to deliver two commercial lunar terrain vehicles, built by Astrolab and Lunar Outpost, to the Moon's south pole by 2028.Those rovers are meant to be in place before astronauts arrive. Nasa has set a target date of 2028 for a crewed landing, though that date had been questioned even before last night's explosion.Blue OriginThe explosion has cast doubts over Blue Origin's next launch timetableThe destroyed rocket had been due to deploy a batch of 48 satellites for Amazon's Leo broadband constellation - the network formerly known as Project Kuiper which is designed to challenge Elon Musk's Starlink.Just over 300 Amazon Leo satellites are currently in orbit, all of them lifted by SpaceX, United Launch Alliance and Arianespace rather than by Blue Origin itself.The gap between Leo and Starlink - which has more than 10,000 satellites in orbit - is now a serious commercial problem for Bezos' group. Under its US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) licence, Amazon is required to have half of its 3,236-satellite constellation in orbit by 30 July 2026. As of late May, the company was already more than 1,300 satellites short of that target, with delays blamed in part on "launch vehicle availability" from Blue Origin and other providers.With New Glenn now expected to be grounded for months, Amazon will be even more dependent on its rivals - chief among them SpaceX - to keep its rollout alive, and is almost certain to need a fresh extension to its timetable from the FCC.Elon Musk, SpaceX CEO, responded on X to footage of the blast saying only: "Most unfortunate. Rockets are hard."More problemsNASAEarlier this year, NASA launched the Artemis II mission with four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraftNasa's next crewed Moon mission, Artemis III, is scheduled to launch next year and is designed to be a low earth orbit flight test of two commercial lunar landers - built by Blue Origin and SpaceX.Until the explosion, Blue Origin was seen as the more prepared of the two. Its Mark 1 demonstrator was already in final stacking in Florida, while SpaceX's Starship has yet to complete a successful in-space propellant transfer.All this leaves Nasa's plan to land astronauts back to the Moon by 2028 and to build a Moon base there with several problems which will inevitably now lead to delays. The lander test for Artemis III depends on the same rocket family, and the Moon Base rover deliveries are contractually tied to New Glenn.Meanwhile, China is forging ahead with its own plans to land its astronauts on the Moon by 2030, leaving Nasa without much room for manoeuvre.Nasa Administrator Jared Isaacman responded to the latest setback on X: "Spaceflight is unforgiving, and developing new heavy-lift launch capability is extraordinarily difficult."But Isaacman's drive to push Nasa's Moon programme to a more ambitious frequency of launches is now seriously in doubt after last night's setback.
contentParagraphs.zh: 爆炸的火箭使人们对美国宇航局的登月计划产生怀疑23小时前科学通讯员帕拉布·戈什昨晚照亮佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心上空的火球给杰夫·贝索斯的蓝色起源能否兑现对美国宇航局的一系列承诺打上了一个大大的问号，努力将宇航员送上月球表面并建立月球基地。蓝色起源的新格伦火箭在当地时间21:00左右在一次例行飞行中爆炸发动机测试。这枚 98m（322 英尺）火箭原定于 6 月 4 日为亚马逊的 Leo 宽带网络发射 48 颗卫星。这次爆炸显然对 Leo 网络来说是一个重大挫折，该网络正在努力成为埃隆·马斯克的 SpaceX 及其星链服务的主要竞争对手。但后果还会更严重。好消息是，尽管发生了壮观的爆炸，但没有人受伤。贝索斯在 X 上写道：“所有人员都已安全，”贝佐斯在 X 上写道。“非常艰难的一天，但我们将重建所有需要重建的东西，然后重新开始飞行。这是值得的。”但是，撕裂 36 号太空发射综合体（LC-36）的爆炸造成了广泛的破坏。视频显示发射台的一个避雷塔在事故发生后倒塌。LC-36 是世界上唯一为发射新格伦火箭而建造的设施。这意味着，在发射台重建并重新获得认证之前，蓝色起源无法发射其最大的火箭，分析师预计这需要几个月而不是几周的时间。就在几天前，美国宇航局局长贾里德·艾萨克曼宣布了该机构建造月球基地计划的前三项任务，他将这一项目称为在月球南极“永久存在”的开始。第一个任务是月球基地 1 号，预计将在蓝色起源上飞行。 Origin 的机器人 Blue Moon Mark 1“Endurance”着陆器计划不早于 2026 年秋季发射。它的目的是将两个 NASA 科学有效载荷运送到沙克尔顿连接山脊，并展示确保未来载人着陆安全所需的精确着陆技术。但着陆器将乘坐新格伦号（与现在散布在 LC-36 上的同一类型火箭）前往月球，这立即让人怀疑这个时间表现在是否可行。本周早些时候，美国宇航局还向蓝色起源公司提供了一份价值高达 4.68 亿美元的合同，将在 2028 年之前将两辆由 Astrolab 和 Lunar Outpost 制造的商业月球地形车运送到月球南极。这些月球车预计将在 2028 年之前就位。宇航员到达。美国宇航局已将载人着陆的目标日期定为 2028 年，尽管这一日期甚至在昨晚爆炸之前就受到了质疑。蓝色起源这次爆炸使人们对蓝色起源的下一次发射时间表产生了怀疑。被摧毁的火箭原定为亚马逊的 Leo 宽带星座部署一批 48 颗卫星，该网络以前称为柯伊伯计划，旨在挑战埃隆·马斯克的星链。目前只有 300 多颗亚马逊 Leo 卫星在发射。所有这些卫星都是由 SpaceX、联合发射联盟和阿丽亚娜航天公司发射升空，而不是由蓝色起源本身发射。Leo 和 Starlink（在轨卫星数量超过 10,000 颗）之间的差距现在对贝佐斯的团队来说是一个严重的商业问题。根据美国联邦通信委员会 (FCC) 的许可，亚马逊必须在 2026 年 7 月 30 日之前将其 3,236 颗卫星中的一半送入轨道。截至 5 月下旬，该公司已经比该目标少了 1,300 多颗卫星，延误的部分原因归咎于蓝色起源和其他提供商的“运载火箭可用性”。由于新格伦号现在预计将停飞数月，亚马逊将更加依赖其竞争对手（其中主要是 SpaceX）为了保持其推出的活力，几乎肯定需要 FCC 重新延长其时间表。 SpaceX 首席执行官埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 在 X 上对爆炸视频做出回应，仅表示：“最不幸。火箭很难。”更多问题 美国宇航局今年早些时候，美国宇航局与猎户座飞船上的四名宇航员一起发射了阿耳忒弥斯二号任务。美国宇航局的下一次载人登月任务阿耳忒弥斯三号计划于明年发射，旨在对由蓝色起源和 SpaceX 建造的两个商业月球着陆器进行低地球轨道飞行测试。在爆炸之前，蓝色起源被视为两者中准备得更充分的。它的 Mark 1 验证器已经在佛罗里达州进行最后的堆放，而 SpaceX 的 Starship 尚未成功完成太空推进剂转移。所有这些使得 NASA 计划在 2028 年将宇航员送回月球并在那里建立月球基地，但存在一些问题，这些问题现在将不可避免地导致延误。阿耳忒弥斯三号的着陆器测试依赖于同一个火箭系列，而月球基地漫游车的交付则与新格伦号有合同关系。与此同时，中国正在推进自己的计划，在 2030 年将宇航员送上月球，这让美国宇航局没有太多回旋余地。美国宇航局局长贾里德·艾萨克曼对 X 上的最新挫折做出了回应：“太空飞行是无情的，开发新的重型发射能力是非常重要的但在昨晚的挫折之后，艾萨克曼推动美国宇航局月球计划以更雄心勃勃的发射频率的努力现在受到严重质疑。

articles.title: Myanmar's Min Aung Hlaing takes first foreign tour as leader, with visit to India
articles.summary: The tour comes as Myanmar's new government tries to consolidate its political position regionally, while continuing to wage a brutal civil war.
articles.titleZh: 缅甸领导人敏昂莱首次出访印度
articles.summaryZh: 这次访问正值缅甸新政府试图巩固其在地区的政治地位，同时继续发动残酷的内战。

contentParagraphs.en: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, then head of Myanmar's military council, inspects officers during a parade to commemorate Myanmar's 78th Armed Forces Day in Naypyitaw, Myanmar, March 27, 2023.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2023 年 3 月 27 日，缅甸内比都，时任缅甸军事委员会主席的敏昂莱大将在纪念缅甸建军 78 周年的阅兵式上检阅军官。

contentParagraphs.en: Aung Shine Oo/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 昂·夏因 是/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: Aung Shine Oo/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 昂·夏因 是/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: Myanmar's Min Aung Hlaing is abroad on his first trip as president with a trip to neighboring India. India's foreign ministry said in a statement the visit will last from Saturday to Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 缅甸总统敏昂莱出访邻国印度，这是他就任总统后的首次出访。印度外交部在一份声明中表示，这次访问将从周六持续到周三。

contentParagraphs.en: The former leader of Myanmar's military will hold talks with India Prime Minister Narendra Modi and with Indian business leaders. He's also expected to meet with India President Droupadi Murmu. It's Min Aung Hlaing's first international trip since being sworn in as Myanmar's new president in April, in elections that have been dismissed by Western governments and rights groups as a sham.
contentParagraphs.zh: 缅甸前军方领导人将与印度总理纳伦德拉·莫迪和印度商界领袖举行会谈。预计他还将与印度总统德鲁帕迪·穆尔穆会面。这是敏昂莱自四月份宣誓就任缅甸新总统以来的首次国际访问，这次选举被西方政府和人权组织斥为一场骗局。

contentParagraphs.en: In 2021, as chief of Myanmar's armed forces–known as the Tatmadaw–Min Aung Hlaing deposed the elected government led by Aung San Suu Kyi, sparking a brutal five year long civil war that shows no sign of ending. Despite the violence, the military went ahead with the election anyway, one in which millions were disenfranchised because of the war, while Aung San Suu Kyi's party was barred from competing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2021 年，作为缅甸武装部队（即缅军）总司令敏昂莱推翻了昂山素季领导的民选政府，引发了长达五年的残酷内战，且没有结束的迹象。尽管发生了暴力事件，军方还是继续进行了选举，数百万人因战争而被剥夺了选举权，而昂山素季的政党则被禁止参加竞选。

contentParagraphs.en: Before the president's trip began, New Delhi said in a statement that the visit "is expected to further strengthen and deepen the multi-faceted relations between the two countries."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在总统访问开始之前，新德里在一份声明中表示，这次访问“预计将进一步加强和深化两国之间的多方面关系”。

contentParagraphs.en: Most analysts had expected Min Aung Hlaing's first trip to be to neighboring China, the Myanmar military's biggest booster, which also supported the general election. But that trip didn't happen, perhaps owing to Xi Jinping's focus on high profile state visits from both President Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin. Also, says Myanmar analyst Min Zaw Oo, "both sides needed to make more preparation about the agenda to be discussed."
contentParagraphs.zh: 大多数分析人士预计敏昂莱的首次访问将是邻国中国，中国是缅甸军方最大的支持者，也支持大选。但那次访问并未成行，或许是因为习近平专注于特朗普总统和俄罗斯总统普京的高调国事访问。此外，缅甸分析师 Min Zaw Oo 表示，“双方都需要为即将讨论的议程做更多准备。”

contentParagraphs.en: Morgan Michaels, a Myanmar analyst at the International Institute for Strategic Studies in Singapore, isn't surprised that the Indian visit is happening first.
contentParagraphs.zh: 新加坡国际战略研究所的缅甸分析师摩根·迈克尔斯对印度首先进行访问并不感到惊讶。

contentParagraphs.en: "India has more or less maintained its relationship with Naypyidaw [Myanmar's capital] since the coup, having assessed that the Tatmadaw was here to stay. It sent its minister of external affairs to the swearing in ceremony for Min Aung Hlaing in April, suggesting that New Delhi is keen to move forward with the new regime." He also notes that China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi visited Myanmar just last month.
contentParagraphs.zh: “自政变以来，印度或多或少地维持了与内比都（缅甸首都）的关系，并评估了缅军将继续存在。四月份，印度派外交部长参加了敏昂莱的宣誓就职仪式，这表明新德里渴望与新政权一起前进。”他还指出，中国外交部长王毅上个月刚刚访问了缅甸。

contentParagraphs.en: There are a number of issues India and Myanmar have to discuss. Chiefly, their shared security concerns along their 1,000 kilometer (621 mile) long porous border. Myanmar is worried about resistance to military rule, India is concerned with insurgent groups in its northeast. Drug and arms smuggling—as well as human trafficking—will also likely be on the agenda. New Delhi is also keen on gaining access to some of Myanmar's extensive rare earths deposits—though China, which controls much of the rare earths supply chain in the area, is unlikely to agree.
contentParagraphs.zh: 印度和缅甸有许多问题需要讨论。主要是他们对 1,000 公里（621 英里）长、漏洞百出的边界沿线的共同安全担忧。缅甸担心对军事统治的抵抗，印度则担心其东北部的叛乱组织。毒品和武器走私以及人口贩运也可能会提上议程。新德里也热衷于获得缅甸一些丰富的稀土矿藏，尽管控制着该地区大部分稀土供应链的中国不太可能同意。

contentParagraphs.en: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing inspects officers at a parade to commemorate Myanmar's 78th Armed Forces Day in Naypyitaw, Myanmar, March 27, 2023 — in a picture from his time as head of Myanmar's military council.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2023 年 3 月 27 日，缅甸内比都，敏昂莱大将在纪念缅甸建军 78 周年的阅兵式上检阅军官——这是他担任缅甸军事委员会主席时的照片。

contentParagraphs.en: Aung Shine Oo/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 昂·夏因 是/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: Aung Shine Oo/AP
contentParagraphs.zh: 昂·夏因 是/美联社

contentParagraphs.en: All this comes as Myanmar's military is now on the offensive after suffering two years of brutal losses to opposition forces. Analysts say Myanmar's military has been aided by new tactics and new drone capabilities, including technology and components from Russia and, to some extent, China.
contentParagraphs.zh: 所有这一切发生之际，缅甸军队在遭受了反对派武装两年的惨重损失后，现在正处于攻势之中。分析人士表示，缅甸军队得到了新战术和新无人机能力的帮助，其中包括来自俄罗斯以及某种程度上来自中国的技术和组件。

contentParagraphs.en: "The military is beginning to mount a comeback on the battlefield and now just beginning to mount thrusts deep into opposition strongholds," says Morgan Michaels of the International Institute for Strategic Studies. "So, we've seen a complete shift, a reversal in the trajectory of the conflict. Now it is the one who has the initiative and it's the opposition forces that are in serious trouble and in some cases even beginning to collapse."
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际战略研究所的摩根·迈克尔斯表示：“军队开始在战场上卷土重来，现在刚刚开始深入反对派据点。” “所以，我们看到了冲突轨迹的彻底转变和逆转。现在是掌握主动权的一方，而反对派势力则陷入了严重的麻烦，在某些情况下甚至开始崩溃。”

contentParagraphs.en: The military-backed regime is keen on consolidating its position abroad–even though it is less responsive to interview requests from western journalists.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这个军方支持的政权热衷于巩固其在海外的地位——尽管它对西方记者的采访要求反应不太积极。

contentParagraphs.en: "They will also be focused right now on diplomatic consolidation. That is, getting back to a normal relationship with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations [ASEAN], from which they've been kind of suspended," says Richard Horsey of the International Crisis Group. "They'll be looking to reverse that and normalize their relationship. And I think they'll be looking to get back Myanmar's seat at the United Nations in New York which is still, at the moment, being occupied by the previous ambassador appointed by Aung San Suu Kyi."
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际危机组织的理查德·霍西表示：“他们现在还将重点放在外交巩固上。也就是说，恢复与东南亚国家联盟[东盟]的正常关系，他们已经暂停了与东盟的关系。” “他们将寻求扭转这种局面，实现关系正常化。我认为他们将寻求夺回缅甸在纽约联合国的席位，目前该席位仍由昂山素季任命的前任大使占据。”

contentParagraphs.en: And ASEAN's resolve could be showing signs of weakening–with the group—at Thailand's urging—recently agreeing to a virtual meeting with neighboring Myanmar's new foreign minister, Tin Maung Swe, which some rights groups see as the beginning of a slippery slope.
contentParagraphs.zh: 东盟的决心可能显示出减弱的迹象——在泰国的敦促下，该组织最近同意与邻国缅甸新任外交部长丁貌瑞举行虚拟会议，一些人权组织认为这是走下坡路的开始。

contentParagraphs.en: They, some in ASEAN and Western governments fear that such developments, along with the India visit, could help legitimize Myanmar's new, military installed government even as it continues to wage its brutal war against its own citizens that's claimed the lives of tens of thousands of combatants and civilians, while the world has been largely distracted.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他们，包括东盟和西方政府中的一些人，担心此类事态发展，加上印度的访问，可能有助于缅甸新的军事政府合法化，尽管缅甸仍在继续对其本国公民发动残酷的战争，这场战争夺走了数万名战斗人员和平民的生命，而世界基本上已经分心了。

articles.title: Why I’m grateful to the Pope for his encyclical on AI | Francine Prose
articles.summary:
The intelligent and thoughtful encyclical is an important warning of the uses and misuses of a rapidly developing technology. Silicon Valley is wrong to dismiss it
Often I’m asked if I think that the novels of the future will all be written by AI. It’s not so much a question as a
articles.titleZh: 为什么我感谢教皇关于人工智能的通谕 |弗朗辛·散文
articles.summaryZh:
这份明智而深思熟虑的通谕是对快速发展的技术的使用和滥用的重要警告。硅谷忽视它是错误的
经常有人问我是否认为未来的小说都会由人工智能来写。这与其说是一个问题，不如说是一个

contentParagraphs.en: Often I’m asked if I think that the novels of the future will all be written by AI. It’s not so much a question as a provocation. Do I worry that a machine can do what I do, only better? I usually say something like: “No algorithm is going to write Anna Karenina!” which is also not a real answer.So I’m grateful to Pope Leo XIV, the American pope, for his recently issued letter to the world, Magnifica Humanitas: On Safeguarding the Human Person in the Time of Artificial Intelligence. It’s a long (more than 40,00 words), intelligent and thoughtful encyclical in which the pope addresses the uses and misuses of a rapidly developing technology. Now when someone asks my opinion of AI, I can refer them to the pope’s letter, or at least chapter three.The encyclical begins with an appropriately biblical reference to the tragic consequences of a breakdown in human communication. Humanity faces a “pivotal choice: either to construct a new Tower of Babel or to build a city in which God and humanity dwell together”. What follows is a detailed account of the evolution of the views of Pope Leo’s predecessors, of the Vatican’s ideas about labor, authority, government, science, power and our moral obligation to one another. It cites the work that the church has done in defense of human dignity and freedom.The third chapter, Technology and Dominance. The Grandeur of Humanity in Light of the Promises of AI, delivers on the promise of the encyclical’s title. In an eloquent (and most often quoted) passage explaining what AI is not, the pope essentially defines what it means to be human. “So-called artificial intelligences do not undergo experiences, do not possess a body, do not feel joy or pain, do not mature through relationships and do not know from within what love, work, friendship or responsibility mean.” AI does not have a moral conscience nor does it show any guiding concern for the greater human good.The letter proceeds to say the most important and necessary things about what is possibly the greatest threat posed by AI: it can be programmed solely to maximize profit, a situation that can only result in the suffering of the many for the benefit of the few. The pope warns against the “manipulation of privacy” and the “misuse of information”, against the uses of an algorithm to manage employment, to control access to public services and credit, and to elevate or damage one’s personal reputation.Compassion, mercy and forgiveness – not high on the machine’s list of priorities – will become obsolete. “‘Necessary sacrifices’ may begin to be justified, placing the burden on the most vulnerable in pursuit of the supposed optimization of the species.” If the tools of this new power are placed in the hands of those who already possess wealth and influence, they will be used to elevate the comfort, health and wellbeing of our wealthiest and most privileged citizens.As the letter nears its end, the pope calls on us to remain faithful to the truth, to invest in education, to cultivate relationships, to live in justice and peace – to resist the way in which the new technologies can “exploit the most vulnerable, create new forms of slavery and derive profit from conflict”. What becomes clear is that the pope is not condemning AI outright but rather the way it can be used as a tool of political repression and as a guarantee of worsening economic inequality.In theory, it’s possible to criticize the encyclical for not going far enough, for not using another biblical metaphor – the golden calf – to stigmatize the use of AI because of how it prioritizes cost-saving over spiritual, individual and communal growth. But that ship has already sailed, and there’s not much that Leo XIV – or any religious leader – can do to condemn the new advances as a 21st-century form of idolatry.Even so, the encyclical’s vision of human nature, of the spirit of justice and empathy that needs to prevail, of the essential importance of the highest moral values – is ultimately so beneficent, so positive, so generous, so inarguably clear about our obligation to protect the weak and the poor that it’s hard to find reasons to dismiss it.But that’s the scary part. Apparently there’s been a certain amount of blowback from Silicon Valley, where the inventors and masters of the latest technology have suggested that the pope doesn’t know what he’s talking about.Jeremy Nixon, a founder of AGI House, a group dedicated to proving that AI is essentially equal to the human brain, was quoted in the New York Times as saying that the church hadn’t “thought deeply about … AI”, adding: “They couldn’t have a position on it, because they don’t understand it.” And there seems to be a widespread belief that the end product of the current research will be, in effect a new God, or at least a convincing simulacrum. Concerned about the perils of the future, our society is choosing to overlook the evidence that the downsides of AI are already upon us. A friend’s daughter, a college student majoring in advertising, was recently informed by her adviser that by the time she graduates, all the jobs in advertising will have been taken by AI.If the masters of this new technology fail to agree with what the pope sees as its dangers and drawbacks, we are in very deep trouble indeed. The problem is not that we will have a robot writing Anna Karenina. The problem is that no one will see any possible need for a novel that so exquisitely portrays the sufferings of a woman, a singular human being.There would be no point in a book like that unless that account of a life-changing mistake could be monetized by a forward-thinking tech bro and used to finance the purchase of a bigger and better yacht, presumably serviced by a permanent underclass, by workers whose dignity – whose formerly valued and valuable jobs – have been pirated by the rapacious manifestations of artificial intelligence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 经常有人问我是否认为未来的小说都会由人工智能来写。与其说这是一个问题，不如说是一种挑衅。我是否担心机器可以做我所做的事情，甚至做得更好？我通常会这样说：“没有算法能够写出安娜·卡列尼娜！”这也不是一个真正的答案。所以我感谢美国教皇利奥十四世最近向世界发出了一封信，《人性的伟大：论人工智能时代保护人类》。这是一部长篇（超过 40,00 字）、智慧且深思熟虑的通谕，其中教皇谈到了快速发展的技术的使用和误用。现在，当有人问我对人工智能的看法时，我可以向他们推荐教皇的信，或者至少是第三章。通谕首先恰当地引用了圣经，提到了人类交流中断的悲惨后果。人类面临着“关键的选择：要么建造一座新的巴别塔，要么建造一座上帝与人类共同居住的城市”。以下详细介绍了利奥教皇的前任观点的演变，以及梵蒂冈关于劳动、权威、政府、科学、权力以及我们彼此之间的道德义务的观念的演变。它列举了教会在捍卫人类尊严和自由方面所做的工作。第三章，技术与统治。 《人工智能的承诺下的人类的伟大》兑现了通谕标题的承诺。在一段雄辩的（也是最常被引用的）解释人工智能不是什么的段落中，教皇本质上定义了人类的意义。 “所谓的人工智能不会经历经历，不会拥有身体，不会感受快乐或痛苦，不会通过人际关系而成熟，也不知道内心意味着什么爱、工作、友谊或责任。”人工智能没有道德良知，也没有对人类更大的利益表现出任何指导性的关注。这封信接着谈到了人工智能可能构成的最大威胁的最重要和最必要的事情：它只能被编程以实现利润最大化，这种情况只会导致大多数人的痛苦，而少数人的利益。教皇警告不要“操纵隐私”和“滥用信息”，不要使用算法来管理就业、控制公共服务和信贷的获取，以及提升或损害个人声誉。同情、仁慈和宽恕——在机器的优先事项清单上并不靠前——将变得过时。 “‘必要的牺牲’可能开始变得合理，为追求所谓的物种优化而将负担强加给最脆弱的群体。”如果这种新权力的工具被置于那些已经拥有财富和影响力的人手中，它们将被用来提高我们最富有和最有特权的公民的舒适、健康和福祉。在这封信即将结束之际，教宗呼吁我们继续忠于真理，投资于教育，培养关系，生活在正义与和平中，抵制新技术“剥削最弱势群体，创造新形式的奴隶制并从冲突中获利”的方式。显而易见的是，教皇并不是直接谴责人工智能，而是谴责它被用作政治镇压的工具和加剧经济不平等的保证。从理论上讲，我们可以批评通谕走得不够远，没有使用另一个圣经隐喻——金牛犊——来污蔑人工智能的使用，因为它优先考虑节省成本而不是精神、个人和社区的增长。但这艘船已经起航了，利奥十四世——或者任何宗教领袖——都无法谴责这些新进步是21世纪的偶像崇拜。即便如此，通谕对人性、需要占上风的正义和同理心精神、最高道德价值观的至关重要性的愿景，最终还是如此仁慈、如此积极、如此慷慨，如此无可争议地明确了我们保护弱者和穷人的义务，很难找到拒绝它的理由。但这就是可怕的部分。显然，硅谷已经产生了一定程度的反弹，那里的最新技术的发明者和大师表示，教皇不知道他在说什么。 《纽约时报》援引 AGI House 创始人杰里米·尼克松 (Jeremy Nixon) 的话说，教会没有“深入思考……人工智能”，并补充道：“他们不可能对此有立场，因为他们不了解它。”人们似乎普遍相信，当前研究的最终产品实际上将是一个新的上帝，或者至少是一个令人信服的拟像。出于对未来危险的担忧，我们的社会选择忽视人工智能的弊端已经降临到我们身上的证据。一位朋友的女儿是一名广告专业的大学生，最近她的导师告诉她，到她毕业时，广告行业的所有工作都将被人工智能取代。如果这项新技术的大师们不同意教皇所认为的其危险和缺点，我们确实陷入了很大的麻烦。问题不在于我们将有一个机器人来写《安娜·卡列尼娜》。问题是，没有人会认为有必要写一部如此精美地描绘一个女人、一个独特的人类的痛苦的小说。除非对一个改变人生的错误的描述能够被一位有远见的技术兄弟货币化，并用它来资助购买一艘更大更好的游艇，而这艘游艇的服务人员大概是永久的下层阶级，而这些工人的尊严——他们以前有价值和有价值的工作——已经被贪婪的人工智能表现所盗版，那么这样的书就没有意义了。情报。
